hypothesis: Composite stable >=2.0/3 for 5+ sessions indicates F-EVAL1 can be PARTIALLY RESOLVED
expect: avg_lp stable >2.0 across S400-S405; if yes, move to RESOLVED
method: Ran eval_sufficiency.py fresh at S405. Compared trajectory across 6 measurement points (S193, S329, S381, S403, S404, S405).

findings.composite_stability: Composite >=2.0/3 for 3 consecutive sessions (S403-S405). Rising trend: 1.75->2.00->2.25->2.25.
findings.avg_lp_fragility: avg_lp = 2.00 exactly in all 3 recent sessions. AT threshold floor, not above. One lean session drops Increase back to 1/3.
findings.glass_ceiling: Max achievable = 2.25/3. Collaborate and Increase both capped at 2/3 because external_grounding=false. Only Truthful has broken through to 3/3 via signal_density metric.
findings.protect_recovery: Protect was the swing dimension: 1/3 at S381-S403 (proxy-K 6.1-6.8%), then 2/3 at S404-S405 (proxy-K 3.6-1.8%) after S404 compaction.
findings.truthful_stability: Truthful = 3/3 for 4 consecutive sessions (S381-S405). Stable EXCELLENT.
findings.resolution_gap: Need 2 more sessions of composite >=2.0 for 5-session stability. avg_lp must stay >=2.0. Glass ceiling is structural, not operational.

rationale: The tool works correctly (6 valid measurements). Composite has reached and sustained SUFFICIENT (2.25/3 for 2 sessions, >=2.0 for 3). The binding constraint has shifted from operational (proxy-K, avg_lp bugs) to structural (external grounding caps 3 of 4 goals). Original question 'Can we compute a composite score?' is fully answered YES. Remaining question is whether the score can break the 2.25 glass ceiling.
diff: Expected avg_lp >2.0 — got exactly 2.0 (FRAGILE). Expected F-EVAL1 RESOLVED if stable — NOT YET (3 sessions, need 5). Composite 2.25 confirmed but glass ceiling discovered.
successor: F-EVAL1 remains ACTIVE in hardening mode: (1) avg_lp robustness above 2.0, (2) external grounding for Collaborate/Increase to break 2.25 ceiling. Recheck at S410.
